policy: Curated from binder 1 (علوم مبدا و معاد). FROZEN during the autonomous KAHSKOLE rollout — new unknown terms emit needs-human-review rather than auto-expanding this file. Curated against Claude's training on Ismaili (Tayyibi) cosmological, philosophical, and prosopographical vocabulary. Confidence is 'high' only when the definition would not change across reasonable Ismaili authorities; lesser-known recent figures are 'medium'.
notes: Many Urdu terms have multiple orthographic variants (ۃ/ہ, etc.); the first-occurrence rule in the reviewer means we'll catch only the variant that appears first in a section. If a section uses two variants, only one annotation is emitted.
Quran-fragment phrases, single letters, and OCR-style spell-outs are deliberately excluded.

terms.definition_ur: ابدا اور خلق سے ماورا الٰہی ذات؛ تمام مُبدَعات کا منبع
terms.definition_en: The Exalted Originator — the absolutely transcendent divine reality, beyond being and non-being, source of all originated beings. The supreme principle in Ismaili emanationist theology.

terms.definition_ur: مبدع تعالی کا ایک تعظیمی صیغہ
terms.definition_en: Honorific variant of al-Mubdi' Ta'ala — 'the Originator, glorified is He'.

terms.definition_ur: عدم محض سے وجود میں لانا، بغیر کسی مادہ یا زمانہ کے
terms.definition_en: Origination from absolute nothingness, without prior matter or time. Distinct from khalq (creation from pre-existing matter). The signature act of al-Mubdi' Ta'ala.

terms.definition_ur: مبدع تعالی کی پہلی پیدائش — روحانی نورانی ذوات
terms.definition_en: The originated beings — the spiritual luminous entities brought forth instantly (dafa'tan wahida) by al-Mubdi'.

terms.definition_ur: مبدع تعالی کی پہلی منبعث ذات؛ تمام عقول و نفوس کا سر چشمہ
terms.definition_en: The First Intellect — the first being emanated by al-Mubdi', the unique direct effect of the Originator and source of all subsequent intellects.

terms.definition_ur: عقل اول کے بعد منبعث ہونے والی ذات، منبعثین میں شامل
terms.definition_en: The Second Intellect — emanated together with the Third Intellect from the First, as one of the two Munba'ithin.

terms.definition_ur: عقل اول سے منبعث دوسرا وجود، منبعثین کا ایک جزو
terms.definition_en: The Third Intellect — emanated together with the Second, the two known collectively as the Munba'ithin (Emanators).

terms.definition_ur: وہ دو ذوات (عقل ثانی و ثالث) جو عقل اول کے بعد ایک ساتھ نکلے
terms.definition_en: The Emanators — the Second and Third Intellects, which proceeded together from the First Intellect in the Ismaili emanationist scheme.

terms.definition_ur: منبعثین میں سے پہلا — عقل ثانی
terms.definition_en: The First Emanator — i.e., the Second Intellect (the senior of the two Munba'ithin).

terms.definition_ur: ایک وجود سے دوسرے وجود کا نکلنا (بغیر کسی فاصلہ کے)
terms.definition_en: Emanation — the procession of one being from another (e.g., Second/Third Intellects from the First), without temporal or spatial gap.

terms.definition_ur: عقل اول کا ابتدائی کمال — اپنے وجود میں مکمل ہونا
terms.definition_en: First Perfection — the First Intellect's primary perfection, its complete actuality at the moment of origination.

terms.definition_ur: عقل اول کا ثانوی کمال — منبعثین کے ذریعے حاصل ہونے والا
terms.definition_en: Second Perfection — the further perfection the First Intellect attains through the emanation of the Munba'ithin.

terms.definition_ur: دس عقول کا دسواں — عالم جسمانی کا مدبر
terms.definition_en: The Tenth (Intellect) — the Active Intellect, demiurge of the physical world in the standard Ismaili decuple-intellect scheme. Often paired with the epithets mudabbir (governor) and hakim (sage).

terms.definition_ur: عاشر کا کامل لقب — مدبر اور حکیم
terms.definition_en: Full epithet of the Tenth Intellect: the Wise Governor — the cosmic principle that orders the world of generation and corruption.
Honorific reference to the Tenth Intellect.

terms.definition_ur: عقل کے بعد آنے والی عالمی روح
terms.definition_en: Universal Soul — the cosmic principle of life proceeding from Intellect, animating the sensible cosmos.
Variant of al-Nafs al-Kulliyya — Universal Soul.

terms.definition_ur: انسان کی بولنے والی، عقل دار روح
terms.definition_en: Rational/Speaking Soul — the highest power of the human soul, capable of intellection.
terms.source_authority: Ibn Sina via Ismaili philosophical tradition

terms.definition_ur: نشو و نما والی نباتی روح
terms.definition_en: Vegetative Soul — the lowest power of the soul, responsible for nutrition, growth, and reproduction.
terms.source_authority: Aristotelian psychology adopted by Ismaili philosophers

terms.definition_ur: حواس اور حرکت والی حیوانی روح
terms.definition_en: Sensitive Soul — the animal-soul power, responsible for sensation and voluntary motion.
terms.source_authority: Aristotelian psychology adopted by Ismaili philosophers

terms.definition_ur: ہوا یا روح کی صورت رکھنے والی نفس
terms.definition_en: Pneumatic Soul — the soul understood as a subtle spirit/breath.

terms.definition_ur: روحانی، ریحی صفات رکھنے والے وجود
terms.definition_en: Pneumatic/spirit-like entities; those possessing the rihiyya quality.
Hyle — prime matter, pure potentiality awaiting form. Adopted from Aristotelian metaphysics.
terms.source_authority: Aristotelian metaphysics, adopted by Ismaili philosophers

terms.definition_en: Variant orthography of hayula — prime matter.
terms.source_authority: Aristotelian metaphysics, adopted by Ismaili philosophers

terms.definition_ur: صلاحیت میں موجود ہونا، فعل میں نہ ہونا
terms.definition_en: Aristotelian technical term: existing potentially, not yet actualized.

terms.definition_ur: فعل میں موجود ہونا، مکمل ہونا
terms.definition_en: Aristotelian technical term: existing actually, fully realized.

terms.definition_ur: وہ شکل جو ہیولی کو مخصوص شے بناتی ہے
terms.definition_en: Form — the principle that actualizes hyle into a specific being (hylomorphism).

terms.definition_ur: کسی شے کی حقیقت یا ماہیت
terms.definition_en: Essence; in Ismaili usage, sometimes also 'self' or 'being'.
Lahut — the divine aspect; the spiritual reality of a being. Paired with nasut (the human aspect). Central to Ismaili Imamology.
Nasut — the human aspect; the embodied human form. Counterpart to lahut.
The natural/physical human aspect — the body in its sensible form.
The particular human aspect — used in Imamological discussions of the Imam's body.
Shadow human aspect — the apparent, contingent human form.
The divine aspect of the Imam — the Imam's lahut, his luminous essence.

terms.definition_ur: تعظیمی صیغہ — امام کی الٰہی حقیقت
terms.definition_en: Honorific phrase for the divine aspect (lahut).
The spiritual world — realm of the originated luminous beings, prior to the physical cosmos.
The world of life — biological/animate realm.
The physical/corporeal world — the sublunar realm of generation and corruption.

terms.definition_ur: بنیادی عناصر یا افلاک کا عالم
terms.definition_en: The world of the Mothers — the realm of the primary elements/spheres that 'mother' the sublunar beings.
Tawhid — affirmation of divine oneness; the foundational doctrine of Islam, given a strongly apophatic reading in Ismaili thought.

terms.english: Tawhid of the Lord of the Worlds
terms.definition_ur: تمام جہانوں کے رب کی توحید
terms.definition_en: Affirming the unity of the Lord of all worlds — the comprehensive form of tawhid.

terms.definition_ur: مبدع تعالی کی توحید — مطلق تنزیہ
terms.definition_en: The tawhid proper to al-Mubdi' Ta'ala — the strictest apophatic affirmation, denying all attributes that would imply createdness.
Divinity — the quality of being a god/object of worship.
Orthographic variant of uluhiyya — divinity.
Divine oneness; the attribute of being singular and unique.

terms.definition_ur: اللہ کو ہر مخلوقی صفت سے پاک قرار دینا
terms.definition_en: Tanzih — declaring God transcendent of all creaturely attributes; apophatic theology. Centrally important in Ismaili thought.
terms.source_authority: Islamic theology; foregrounded in Ismaili apophasis

terms.definition_ur: ہر صفت اور تعلق سے علیحدہ کرنا
terms.definition_en: Tajrid — abstraction/stripping away of all attributes from God; companion to tanzih.

terms.definition_ur: رتبہ کا نام — امام کا قریبی نائب
terms.definition_en: Bab (Gate) — the highest rank in the Ismaili da'wa hierarchy beneath the Imam, the Imam's closest deputy.
Gate of Gates — the supreme Bab, head of the gate-rank.
The inner/esoteric Bab — interior aspect of the gate-rank.

terms.definition_ur: انبعاث کا باب — روحانی نکاس کا دروازہ
terms.definition_en: The emanative gate — channel of spiritual emanation.

terms.definition_ur: دعوت کا ایک رتبہ — امام کی گواہی دینے والا
terms.definition_en: Hujja (Proof) — a rank in the Ismaili da'wa hierarchy below the Imam and Bab, bearing witness to the Imam.
Hujjas of the Night — hidden/concealed proofs; one of the categories of hujja-rank in the hierarchy (paired with nahari, of the day).

terms.definition_ur: دعوت کے درجات — حدِ ولی، حدِ مستجیب وغیرہ
terms.definition_en: Hudud — the graded ranks of the da'wa hierarchy; literally 'limits/boundaries'.
Da'i — caller/missionary; a senior rank in the da'wa hierarchy, charged with summoning believers.

terms.definition_ur: دعوت کا جواب دینے والا — مبتدی مومن
terms.definition_en: Mustajib — the responder; an initiate who has answered the da'wa, the lowest rank of inducted believers.

terms.definition_ur: ناطق کے بعد آنے والا وصی
terms.definition_en: Wasi — Legatee; the rank that succeeds the Natiq in each prophetic cycle. Ali is the Wasi of Muhammad.

terms.definition_ur: نبی، جس نے کسی دور میں شریعت بیان کی
terms.definition_en: Natiq — Speaker; a law-giving prophet who opens a cosmic cycle. The Six Natiqs are Adam, Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, Muhammad; the Seventh is the Qa'im.
Honorific reference to the Natiq.

terms.english: The Qa'im (peace be upon his memory)
terms.definition_ur: ساتویں دور کا قائم — وقت کا منتظر
terms.definition_en: The Qa'im — the eschatological Imam-Riser who closes the cycle of concealment and inaugurates the cycle of unveiling.
Imam — the divinely-designated guide of the believers, central to Ismaili (and Shi'i) theology.
terms.source_authority: Shi'i theology, central to Ismaili tradition

terms.definition_en: Honorific reference to the Imam.
Imamate — the office/institution of the Imam.
The Imami haykal — the Imam's spiritual form/temple.

terms.definition_ur: خفیہ، باطنی طور پر کی گئی نص
terms.definition_en: Hidden designation — esoteric nass (formal appointment) of an Imam, in contrast to the open/public nass.
Triad of spiritual ranks — Legatee, Imam, and Caller — recurring grouping in Ismaili hierarchical doctrine.

terms.definition_ur: تین روحانی اصول — جد (سیربلندی)، فتح (کشاد)، خیال (تصور)
terms.definition_en: The three spiritual principles — Jadd (eminence), Fath (opening), Khayal (imagination) — three intermediate causes in the Ismaili emanationist scheme, often identified with the Pen, Tablet, and Angel of Revelation.
terms.source_authority: Tayyibi cosmology (Hamid al-Din al-Kirmani and successors)

terms.definition_en: Parenthesized form of the Jadd-Fath-Khayal triad.
Mulhim — the Inspirer; the spiritual principle that conveys inspiration to lower ranks.
Inspirer and Teacher — pair of cosmic-pedagogical functions.

terms.definition_ur: ہر چیز میں جاری اور سرایت کرنے والا علم
terms.definition_en: Pervading and current knowledge — divine knowledge that flows through and saturates all things.

terms.english: Knowledge of what was and shall be
terms.definition_ur: ماضی، حال اور مستقبل کا علم
terms.definition_en: Knowledge of what was and what shall be — a divine/imami attribute denoting comprehensive temporal knowledge.
Divine sciences — the theological/metaphysical sciences as understood in Ismaili thought.

terms.definition_ur: خطا اور گناہ سے محفوظ ہونا
terms.definition_en: 'Isma — infallibility/divine protection from error and sin; an attribute of prophets and Imams.
Universal infallibility — the complete form of 'isma attributed to the Imam.
'Abd — servant/slave of God.
Ma'bud — the object of worship; God.
'Ubudiyya — the state and act of being a servant of God.
'Abdiyya — the condition of being a servant; variant of 'ubudiyya.

terms.english: All at once (in a single instant)
terms.definition_en: All in a single instant — the manner in which the spiritual world was originated (without temporal succession).

terms.definition_ur: ہمیشہ سے ہونا، شروع نہ ہونا
terms.definition_en: Azaliyya — pre-eternity; the state of having no beginning.
Radiant/effulgent light — a high-degree luminosity attributed to spiritual beings.

terms.definition_ur: ہر چیز میں سرایت کرنے والا نور
terms.definition_en: Pervading light — light that flows into and saturates everything.
Light of the Word — the spiritual light of the kalima (testimony of faith).

terms.definition_ur: اقرار کا کلمہ — لا الہ الا اللہ
terms.definition_en: Kalima — the word; specifically the shahada testifying to God's oneness.
The testimony of unity: 'There is no god but Allah'.

terms.english: There is no god but Allah
terms.definition_en: The first half of the shahada — the foundational testimony of monotheism.
Luminous bodies — the spiritual luminous forms of originated beings.
Haykal — form/temple; the spiritual or bodily form of a being. In Ismaili usage especially the form of the Imam.
Luminous form — a haykal of light.
Plural of haykal — forms/temples.

terms.definition_ur: روحانی عالم کا ابلیس — اصلی، روحانی ضد
terms.definition_en: Spiritual Iblis — the cosmic adversary at the spiritual level, the original opponent of the First Intellect in Ismaili cosmology.

terms.definition_ur: وہ روحانی ذوات جو نادم ہوئیں
terms.definition_en: The Regretful — a class of spiritual beings who turned in regret, contrasted with the obstinate.

terms.definition_ur: وقتی طور پر رکھے گئے ذوات
terms.definition_en: The Deposited — beings entrusted with a temporary station (e.g., an Imam-mustawda holds the Imamate as deputy).
The Established — beings in a permanent station; counterpart to mustawda'in.
The Veiled — those who are concealed behind hijab.
Veiled — singular of muhtajibin.
Hijab — veil; a barrier between spiritual ranks.
Veils — plural of hijab.
Doubting and perplexed — a moral category of souls (paired class).
Plural — doubting and perplexed ones.

terms.definition_ur: گناہ پر اڑے رہنے والے، تکبر کرنے والے
terms.definition_en: Insistent (on wrongdoing) and arrogant — a category of damned souls.
Singular form.
Orthographic variant.
The Repentant — those who turn back to God.

terms.definition_ur: سیدھے راستے پر چلنے والے مومن
terms.definition_en: Believers travelling the straight path — the saved class.
Khatima bi'l-khayr — to die in a state of grace; a 'good ending'.

terms.definition_ur: قبر میں سوال کرنے والے دو فرشتے
terms.definition_en: Munkar and Nakir — the two angels who interrogate the deceased in the grave.
The Ka'ba — God's House at Mecca.
The spiritual Ka'ba — esoteric counterpart of the earthly Ka'ba in Ismaili cosmology.
The Sacred Precincts — the realm of holiness, often the abode of the saved spirits.
The Garden of Refuge — one of the Quranic gardens of paradise.
Dar al-Salam — Abode of Peace; a Quranic name for paradise.
'Arsh — the divine Throne, the highest cosmological station in Islamic cosmology; in Ismaili thought, often identified with the Universal Soul or higher Intellects.

terms.definition_ur: تمام افلاک کو گھیرنے والا فلک
terms.definition_en: The Encompassing Sphere — the outermost celestial sphere that contains all others.
The Sphere of the Moon — the innermost celestial sphere; boundary between sublunar (corruptible) and supralunar (eternal) worlds.

terms.definition_ur: فلک قمر کے نیچے — مادی، فانی دنیا
terms.definition_en: The sublunar realm — the world of generation and corruption below the sphere of the Moon.
Aflak — the celestial spheres of Aristotelian/Ptolemaic astronomy.
Wandering stars — the planets, in contrast to the fixed stars.
Sphere of Air — one of the sublunar elemental spheres.
Sphere of Water — sublunar elemental sphere.
Sphere of Intense Cold — Zamharir, a cold celestial region.
Sphere of the Earth — the central elemental sphere.
Sphere of Aether — the fifth element, of which the celestial spheres are composed.
The Three Generated — the three sublunar kingdoms generated from the elements: minerals, plants, and animals.
Orthographic variant — the three generated kingdoms.
The Mothers — the primary elements/celestial spheres from which the sublunar 'children' (muwallidat) are generated.
Mizaj — the constitutional mixture (e.g., of the four humors); temperament.
Compound — blended/mixed substance.
Blended temperament — a compound mixture of qualities.
Innate heat — the constitutive vital heat in classical/medieval medicine (Galen, Ibn Sina).
Variant orthography.

terms.definition_ur: نطفہ — جنینی پیدائش کا ابتدائی مرحلہ
terms.definition_en: Nutfa — drop/sperm; the first Quranically-named stage of human embryogenesis.
Mudgha — a chewed-flesh-like clot; a Quranic embryological stage.
Principal organs — the chief organs (brain, heart, liver) in classical/medieval medicine.
Spiritual Adam — the originated/archetypal Adam of the spiritual world, contrasted with the corporeal Adam.
Adam of the Beginning — the first Natiq, opening the present cycle.

terms.definition_ur: کلی انسان — تمام انسانیت کا روحانی نمونہ
terms.definition_en: Universal Human — the cosmic archetype of humanity.
Allah — the proper name of God.
Ilah — the generic word 'god/deity'; root of Allah.
Allahumma — vocative invocation: 'O Allah'.
Bismillah — 'In the name of Allah'; opening invocation.
Rabb — Lord/Sustainer.

terms.definition_ur: پہلے امام، خلیفہ چہارم، آنحضرت ﷺ کے داماد و چچا زاد
terms.definition_en: Ali ibn Abi Talib — first Shi'i Imam, Wasi of the Prophet Muhammad, fourth Sunni caliph; cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet.

terms.term: علی ابن ابی طالب علیہ السلام
terms.english: Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him)
terms.definition_ur: علی ابن ابی طالب کا تعظیمی صیغہ
terms.definition_en: Honorific reference to Imam 'Ali.
Honorific Shi'i name for Imam 'Ali — 'Our Lord Ali'.

terms.definition_ur: علی ابن ابی طالب کا تعظیمی صیغہ
terms.definition_en: Honorific Urdu reference to Imam 'Ali.
Honorific reference to Imam 'Ali.

terms.definition_ur: امام علی کا لقب — مومنوں کا امیر
terms.definition_en: Commander of the Faithful — the principal title of Imam 'Ali.
Orthographic variant — Commander of the Faithful.

terms.definition_ur: حضرت علی کا لقب — حیدر کرار
terms.definition_en: Haydar — 'Lion'; an epithet of Imam 'Ali.
Our Lady Fatima — Fatima al-Zahra, daughter of the Prophet and wife of 'Ali.

terms.definition_ur: بی بی فاطمہ کا اردو تعظیمی صیغہ
terms.definition_en: Urdu honorific 'Lady Fatima' — Fatima al-Zahra.

terms.definition_ur: حضرت علی کے بیٹے، شہید کربلا
terms.definition_en: Imam al-Husayn ibn 'Ali — third Shi'i Imam, martyred at Karbala (61 AH).

terms.definition_ur: حضرت علی کے دو بیٹے، دوسرا اور تیسرا امام
terms.definition_en: Imams al-Hasan and al-Husayn — the two sons of 'Ali and Fatima; second and third Shi'i Imams.

terms.term: امام اسماعیل بن جعفر الصادق علیہ السلام
terms.transliteration: al-Imam Isma'il ibn Ja'far al-Sadiq 'alayhi al-salam
terms.definition_ur: امام جعفر الصادق کے بڑے بیٹے — اسماعیلیوں کے امام
terms.definition_en: Imam Isma'il ibn Ja'far al-Sadiq — elder son of Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq; eponymous Imam of the Isma'ili branch.
Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq — sixth Shi'i Imam; father of Imam Isma'il.
Prophet Ibrahim — Abraham, one of the six Natiqs in Ismaili cyclical history.
Honorific reference to Prophet Ibrahim.

terms.definition_ur: حضرت ابراہیم — خلیل اللہ کا لقب
terms.definition_en: Hazrat Ibrahim Khalil Allah — Abraham, 'Friend of God' (Quran 4:125).

terms.definition_ur: حضرت علی کے والد، آنحضرت ﷺ کے چچا
terms.definition_en: Abu Talib — father of Imam 'Ali, paternal uncle and protector of the Prophet Muhammad; revered in Shi'i tradition as a believer.
'Abd al-Muttalib — paternal grandfather of the Prophet Muhammad.

terms.term: سیدنا حمید الدین کرمانی اعلی اللہ قدسہ
terms.definition_ur: گیارہویں صدی کے اسماعیلی فلسفی، صاحبِ راحت العقل
terms.definition_en: Hamid al-Din al-Kirmani (d. ca. 411/1020) — major Fatimid-era Ismaili philosopher; author of Rahat al-'Aql, foundational text of the decuple-intellect system.

terms.definition_ur: گیارہویں صدی کے داعی الدعاۃ، فاطمی دور
terms.definition_en: al-Mu'ayyad fi'l-Din al-Shirazi (d. 470/1078) — chief Da'i (Da'i al-Du'at) of the Fatimid Imam-Caliph al-Mustansir; major Ismaili theologian.

terms.definition_ur: تیرہویں ہجری صدی کے داعی مطلق (دائودی بہرہ)
terms.definition_en: Sayyidna 'Ali ibn Sulayman — Tayyibi Isma'ili Da'i al-Mutlaq.

terms.term: سیدنا حسن بن ھبۃ اللہ صاحب
terms.definition_ur: تیرہویں ہجری صدی کے دائودی بہرہ داعی
terms.definition_en: Sayyidna Hasan ibn Hibatullah — Tayyibi Da'i al-Mutlaq.

terms.term: سیدنا علی بن علی اعلی اللہ قدسہ
terms.definition_ur: تیرہویں ہجری صدی کے دائودی بہرہ داعی
terms.definition_en: Sayyidna 'Ali ibn 'Ali — Tayyibi Da'i al-Mutlaq.

terms.term: سیدنا سلیمان صاحب أعلی اللہ قدسہ
terms.definition_en: Sayyidna Sulayman — Tayyibi Da'i al-Mutlaq.
Abu Bakr al-Siddiq — first Sunni caliph; companion of the Prophet.
'The Cursed Ibn Muljam' — 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Muljam al-Muradi, Kharijite assassin of Imam 'Ali (40 AH).

terms.definition_ur: کربلا میں امام حسین کا قاتل
terms.definition_en: Shimr ibn Dhi'l-Jawshan — Umayyad commander complicit in the killing of Imam al-Husayn at Karbala.
'Abd Allah ibn Rawaha — companion and poet of the Prophet; martyred at the Battle of Mu'ta (8 AH).
Kaymurt the Great — a legendary primordial figure (the Kayumars of Iranian tradition), incorporated into some Ismaili cyclical schemes.
Honorific reference to Kaymurt.

terms.term: رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ و آلہ وسلم
terms.definition_en: Prophet Muhammad — 'Messenger of Allah, may Allah's blessings be upon him and his family'.
Prophet Muhammad — abbreviated salawat form.

terms.definition_ur: فاطمی خلافت کا بانی — عبید اللہ المہدی
terms.definition_en: Mawlana al-Mahdi — 'Abd Allah/'Ubayd Allah al-Mahdi (d. 322/934), founder of the Fatimid Caliphate, first Fatimid Imam-Caliph.
Parenthesized form of Mawlana al-Mahdi.
The letter mim — in Ismaili esotericism often a symbol for Muhammad and the prophetic cycle.

terms.definition_ur: عربی حرف عین — رمزی معنی، علی کا اشارہ
terms.definition_en: The letter 'ayn — in Ismaili esotericism a symbol for 'Ali; also meaning 'eye' or 'essence'.

terms.definition_ur: محمد ﷺ اور علی علیہ السلام کے رموز
terms.definition_en: The letters Mim and 'Ayn — esoteric symbols for Muhammad and 'Ali.
The Lesser Cycle — shorter cyclical unit in Ismaili history (cf. Greater Cycle).
The Greater Cycle — larger cyclical unit in Ismaili sacred history.

terms.definition_ur: مستوری کا دور — جب امام غائب ہو
terms.definition_en: The Cycle of Concealment — periods when the Imam is hidden (Tayyibi tradition is currently in such a cycle).

terms.definition_ur: ظاہر کا دور — جب امام ظاہر ہو
terms.definition_en: The Cycle of Unveiling — periods when the Imam is manifest; counterpart to dawr al-satr.
Akwar — plural of kawr; the great cosmic cycles.
Jaza'ir — the twelve 'islands' or geographical regions of the Ismaili Da'wa.
Sharpness of insight — depth and acuity of spiritual perception.
The higher/nobler view — the elevated perspective; contrast with nazar adwan.
The lower/baser view — perspectives toward the inferior; contrast with nazar ashraf.

terms.definition_ur: اپنی توبہ سے توبہ کر لیا
terms.definition_en: 'Repented his repentance' — phrase describing those who turn back from their initial turn-back (recidivism).
Ibtida' — the act of beginning; the cosmic origination.
The originative leaven — the primal 'leavening principle' of the originated world in Tayyibi cosmology.
Leaven of Regret — counterpart principle in the cosmogonic story of the Third Intellect's fall and regret.
Urdu-mixed variant of khamira al-nadama.
The originative body — a Tayyibi cosmological notion for the primordial collective body of the originated.
Evil forms — corrupt/depraved spiritual forms; counterpart to surat sharifa.
Noble forms — pure spiritual forms; counterpart to surat khabitha.
Subordinated/serviceable forms — forms in a subaltern relation.
The Two Knots — spiritual knots; figurative ties between cosmic levels.
Walad Tamm — the complete/perfect child; sometimes used for a fully realized spiritual being.

terms.definition_ur: حد قائم کرنے والا یا حد میں آنے والا
terms.definition_en: Muhaddad — the delimited/bounded entity (or, depending on context, the delimiter).
Hadarat — plural of hadra; spiritual 'presences' or honorifically, the dignitaries.
Asas — Foundation; in Ismaili cyclical doctrine, an alternative title for the Wasi (Legatee) of a Natiq.
